questions.question: Which piece of equipment does the material recommend using to check the core temperature of food rather than relying on guesswork?
options: Your hand held over the food

questions.question: The guidance says cooked food should be cooled quickly, ideally within what time, before it is refrigerated?
According to the material, how many times may food be safely reheated?
Which independent government body is responsible for protecting public health in relation to food?
options: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE)

questions.question: After sickness or diarrhoea, how long should a food handler stay away from handling food once symptoms have stopped?
Food safety in England is underpinned by the Food Safety Act 1990 together with which set of regulations?
options: The Health and Safety at Work Act 1974

questions.question: The NHS Eatwell Guide recommends aiming for at least how many portions of fruit and vegetables a day?
The NHS suggests most adults aim for around how many drinks a day?
Who assesses a person's swallowing and sets the texture-modified or thickened-fluid level they need?
Which of the following is described in the material as a high-risk food that readily supports bacterial growth?
Which of the following is named in the material as a harmful bacterium that commonly causes food poisoning?
How should a food handler cover a cut before handling food?
options: With a brightly coloured waterproof dressing
Leave it uncovered so it can breathe

questions.question: The MUST screening tool was developed by which organisation?
In the MUST tool, which overall score indicates that a person is at high risk of malnutrition?
Older people often feel thirst less strongly, so they can become dehydrated without realising it.
Food can be safely reheated more than once as long as it is piping hot each time.
A use-by date relates to food quality, so the food is usually still safe to eat shortly after that date.
The IDDSI framework numbers its levels from 0 to 7, with colour codes and names.
Which of the following are part of the FSA's 4 Cs of food safety? (Select all that apply)
Which of the following are described as high-risk foods that need extra care? (Select all that apply)
Which of the following are benefits of good nutrition and hydration named in the material? (Select all that apply)
options: Increase the risk of pressure ulcers
Support skin integrity and wound healing

questions.question: Which of the following are ways to encourage a person to drink more fluids? (Select all that apply)
options: Offer drinks regularly throughout the day
Offer foods such as soup and jelly
Keep an accessible cup and jug within easy reach

questions.question: Hot food being served should be held at a temperature of ___C or above to keep it out of the danger zone.
Cooked food should be cooled quickly, ideally within ___ minutes, and then refrigerated.
Harmful bacteria multiply most quickly between ___C and 63C, a range known as the danger zone.
